log reg binary h1

Elena C

2023-11-29

H1 states: “There is an effect of scene category (i.e., a difference between images showing man-made vs. natural environments) on the amplitude of the N1 component, i.e. the first major negative EEG voltage deflection.”

The data table included in the analysis contains info on the software, high-pass cutoff values, reference, sampling rate, number of excluded subjects, baseline window length, topographic region, number of channels included in the region, multiple comparisons method, statistical method, and whether the finding was significant or not for the h1 testing.

Load packages

rm(list = ls())
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(readxl)

Load data and inspect it for typos

data <- read_excel("/Users/ecesnaite/Desktop/BuschLab/EEGManyPipelines/git/EEGManyPipelines-personal/Matlab/Big Analysis/initial_variables_h1_pval.xlsx")
data <- as.data.frame(data)
#correct values
table(data$software)
## 
##          besa    brainstorm   brainvision        custom        eeglab 
##             1             5            12             3            44 
## eeglab_erplab   eeglab_limo     fieldtrip     mnepython         other 
##            13             1            29            43            13 
##             R           spm 
##             1             3
data$software[data$software== 'eeglab_erplab'] <- 'eeglab'
data$software[data$software== 'eeglab_limo'] <- 'eeglab'

data <- data[-which(data$mc_method_h1=='rft'),]#high multicollinearity with spm
#table(data$hf_cutoff)
#table(data$software)
#table(data$result_h1)
#hist(data$pval)
#hist(data$pval[data$pval<0.05])

Inspect the data for outliers

Since these are real life preprocessing practices, should we consider possible outliers?

hf_out <- boxplot(data$hf_cutoff)$out
data$hf_cutoff[which(data$hf_cutoff == hf_out)] <- mean((data$hf_cutoff), na.rm = T)

Inspect for normality

# Select only numeric predictors
data <- data[,c(-1,-14)] #delete the row numbers and pvalues of hypothesis testing
data <- na.omit(data)
mynumericdata <- data %>%
  dplyr::select_if(is.numeric) 

shapiro.test(mynumericdata$hf_cutoff)#no data is normal
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  mynumericdata$hf_cutoff
## W = 0.74908, p-value = 1.931e-15
#normalise numeric data
for (i in 1:length(data)) {
  
  if (is.numeric(data[,i])){
    data[,i] <- data[,i]-mean(data[,i])/sd(data[,i])
  }
}
# scaled_data <- mutate_if(data, is.numeric, scale)
#table(scaled_data$pval)
#hist(data$result_h1)
#hist(data$hf_cutoff-mean(data$hf_cutoff)/sd(data$hf_cutoff))

Build a logistic model

model <- glm(result_h1 ~., data = data, family = 'binomial')
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
# Summarize the model
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = result_h1 ~ ., family = "binomial", data = data)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##      Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max  
## -2.58501   0.00000   0.05895   0.43796   2.13587  
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept)                               4.683e+01  1.796e+04   0.003  0.99792
## softwarebrainstorm                       -2.305e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99896
## softwarebrainvision                      -1.928e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99913
## softwarecustom                           -1.981e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99911
## softwareeeglab                           -1.977e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99911
## softwarefieldtrip                        -1.857e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99916
## softwaremnepython                        -1.952e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99912
## softwareother                            -1.517e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99932
## softwareR                                 8.685e-01  2.507e+04   0.000  0.99997
## hf_cutoff                                 1.005e+00  1.114e+00   0.902  0.36698
## rerefavg mastoids                        -3.078e+00  1.691e+00  -1.820  0.06870
## rerefmastoid                             -2.918e+00  1.169e+00  -2.496  0.01255
## rereforiginal                             6.177e-01  1.199e+00   0.515  0.60639
## rerefother                               -2.063e+00  1.576e+00  -1.309  0.19058
## rerefunknown                              2.971e+00  2.487e+00   1.195  0.23219
## ds_fs                                     6.048e-03  2.852e-03   2.120  0.03398
## subj_excluded                             3.501e-01  2.097e-01   1.669  0.09512
## bs_window                                -4.521e-03  1.723e-03  -2.625  0.00867
## topo_region_h1central                    -3.744e+00  3.614e+00  -1.036  0.30022
## topo_region_h1central-parietal-occipital -2.356e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99894
## topo_region_h1centro-parietal             1.843e+01  1.773e+04   0.001  0.99917
## topo_region_h1frontal                    -1.729e+00  2.734e+00  -0.632  0.52722
## topo_region_h1frontal-occipital           1.819e+01  1.250e+04   0.001  0.99884
## topo_region_h1fronto-central             -2.428e+00  2.400e+00  -1.011  0.31183
## topo_region_h1occipital                  -3.701e+00  2.751e+00  -1.345  0.17856
## topo_region_h1occipito-parietal          -3.379e-01  2.317e+00  -0.146  0.88403
## topo_region_h1parietal                   -5.422e+00  3.094e+00  -1.753  0.07966
## topo_region_h1posterior                  -2.330e+01  1.773e+04  -0.001  0.99895
## topo_region_h1unknown                     7.160e-01  2.487e+00   0.288  0.77341
## nr_chan_h1                                7.008e-02  7.024e-02   0.998  0.31842
## time_window_interest_h1                  -4.505e-03  1.508e-03  -2.987  0.00282
## mc_method_h1bonferroni                   -7.828e+00  4.994e+03  -0.002  0.99875
## mc_method_h1holm-bonferroni              -3.186e+00  1.796e+04   0.000  0.99986
## mc_method_h1none                         -2.915e+01  2.858e+03  -0.010  0.99186
## mc_method_h1other                         1.499e+00  5.998e+03   0.000  0.99980
## mc_method_h1permutation                  -2.518e+01  2.858e+03  -0.009  0.99297
## mc_method_h1unknown                      -2.743e+01  2.858e+03  -0.010  0.99234
## stat_method_h1one_sample_ttest            1.172e+00  1.858e+00   0.631  0.52814
## stat_method_h1other                       7.660e-01  1.859e+00   0.412  0.68024
## stat_method_h1pair_ttest                  2.911e+00  1.848e+00   1.575  0.11521
## stat_method_h1rm_anova                    4.101e+00  2.294e+00   1.788  0.07377
##                                            
## (Intercept)                                
## softwarebrainstorm                         
## softwarebrainvision                        
## softwarecustom                             
## softwareeeglab                             
## softwarefieldtrip                          
## softwaremnepython                          
## softwareother                              
## softwareR                                  
## hf_cutoff                                  
## rerefavg mastoids                        . 
## rerefmastoid                             * 
## rereforiginal                              
## rerefother                                 
## rerefunknown                               
## ds_fs                                    * 
## subj_excluded                            . 
## bs_window                                **
## topo_region_h1central                      
## topo_region_h1central-parietal-occipital   
## topo_region_h1centro-parietal              
## topo_region_h1frontal                      
## topo_region_h1frontal-occipital            
## topo_region_h1fronto-central               
## topo_region_h1occipital                    
## topo_region_h1occipito-parietal            
## topo_region_h1parietal                   . 
## topo_region_h1posterior                    
## topo_region_h1unknown                      
## nr_chan_h1                                 
## time_window_interest_h1                  **
## mc_method_h1bonferroni                     
## mc_method_h1holm-bonferroni                
## mc_method_h1none                           
## mc_method_h1other                          
## mc_method_h1permutation                    
## mc_method_h1unknown                        
## stat_method_h1one_sample_ttest             
## stat_method_h1other                        
## stat_method_h1pair_ttest                   
## stat_method_h1rm_anova                   . 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 167.405  on 163  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  79.479  on 123  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 161.48
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 19

Time window of interest and baseline window length shows significant resutls (p<0.01). However, the effect seems to be influenced by a few extreme values.

# # plot the time window effect
data %>%
  mutate(prob = ifelse(result_h1 == "TRUE", 1, 0)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(time_window_interest_h1, prob)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial")) +
  labs(
    title = "Logistic Regression Model",
    x = "Time window",
    y = "Probability of confirming h1"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# plot the baseline window effect
data %>%
  mutate(prob = ifelse(result_h1 == "TRUE", 1, 0)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(bs_window, prob)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial")) +
  labs(
    title = "Logistic Regression Model",
    x = "Baseline window",
    y = "Probability of confirming h1"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Assessing model accuracy

The model accuracy is measured as the proportion of observations that have been correctly classified. Inversely, the classification error is defined as the proportion of observations that have been misclassified.

# Make predictions
probabilities <- model %>% predict(data, type = "response")
head(probabilities)
##         1         2         3         4         5         6 
## 0.7579067 0.9921547 0.9026627 1.0000000 0.9837224 1.0000000

Logistic regression diagnostics

predictors <- colnames(mynumericdata)

# Bind the logit and tidying the data for plot
mynumericdata <- mynumericdata %>%
  mutate(logit = log(probabilities/(1-probabilities))) %>%
  gather(key = "predictors", value = "predictor.value", -logit)

#create scatterplots to inspect linearity
ggplot(mynumericdata, aes(logit, predictor.value))+
  geom_point(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  facet_wrap(~predictors, scales = "free_y") # doesn't look good
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

plot(model, which = 4, id.n = 3) # one extreme value that drags the effect

# Extract model results
model.data <- augment(model) %>% 
  mutate(index = 1:n())

Check for outliers

outlier <- model.data %>% top_n(3, .cooksd) # The data for the top 3 largest values, according to the Cook’s distance
# plot standardized residuals
ggplot(model.data, aes(index, .std.resid)) + 
  geom_point(aes(color = result_h1), alpha = .5) +
  theme_bw()
## Warning: Removed 6 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

model.data %>% 
  filter(abs(.std.resid) > 3)
## # A tibble: 2 × 20
##   .rownames result…¹ softw…² hf_cu…³ reref ds_fs subj_…⁴ bs_wi…⁵ topo_…⁶ nr_ch…⁷
##   <chr>     <lgl>    <chr>     <dbl> <chr> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <chr>     <dbl>
## 1 23        FALSE    brains…  -0.875 avg    509.  -0.429    98.9 occipi…    6.94
## 2 53        TRUE     brains…  -0.875 avg    509.  -0.429   199.  occipi…    3.94
## # … with 10 more variables: time_window_interest_h1 <dbl>, mc_method_h1 <chr>,
## #   stat_method_h1 <chr>, .fitted <dbl>, .resid <dbl>, .hat <dbl>,
## #   .sigma <dbl>, .cooksd <dbl>, .std.resid <dbl>, index <int>, and abbreviated
## #   variable names ¹​result_h1, ²​software, ³​hf_cutoff, ⁴​subj_excluded,
## #   ⁵​bs_window, ⁶​topo_region_h1, ⁷​nr_chan_h1

Colinearity

library(car)
## Loading required package: carData
## 
## Attaching package: 'car'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     recode
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     some
car::vif(model)#high colinearity
##                              GVIF Df GVIF^(1/(2*Df))
## software                 5.966553  8        1.118105
## hf_cutoff                1.997337  1        1.413272
## reref                   17.751168  5        1.333284
## ds_fs                    1.948810  1        1.395998
## subj_excluded            2.311668  1        1.520417
## bs_window                2.423118  1        1.556637
## topo_region_h1          63.259003 11        1.207450
## nr_chan_h1               2.553318  1        1.597911
## time_window_interest_h1  2.617331  1        1.617817
## mc_method_h1             7.029497  6        1.176460
## stat_method_h1          16.089211  4        1.415197
#inspect which variables are correlated
alias( model ) # software spm
## Model :
## result_h1 ~ software + hf_cutoff + reref + ds_fs + subj_excluded + 
##     bs_window + topo_region_h1 + nr_chan_h1 + time_window_interest_h1 + 
##     mc_method_h1 + stat_method_h1
